title-group.article-title: Quaternion Lifting for Multichannel RF and Sensing Signals: A Canonical Lane Representation for Quaternionic Signal Processing

abstract.p: This paper defines a canonical quaternion lifting procedure for multichannel RF and sensing signals whose external intake remains standard complex-channel data. Given two complex channels z1[n]=a[n]+ib[n] and z2[n]=c[n]+id[n], the lift forms a quaternion lane q[n]=z1[n]+z2[n]j = a[n]+b[n]i+c[n]j+d[n]k. For larger captures, channels are grouped deterministically into quaternion lanes Q[g,n] in H. The paper formalizes this lift, its inverse unpacking map, odd-channel handling, and representation-level invariants that make it suitable as a canonical internal form. The contribution is representational rather than speculative: the lift preserves paired phase-bearing relations inside a single quaternion object while remaining exactly recoverable back to grouped complex channels. This establishes a deterministic, information-preserving front end for downstream quaternionic signal processing.

license.license-p: This is an open-access article distributed under the terms of the Creative Commons Attribution License (CC BY 4.0).

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: The paper introduces a canonical grouped quaternion lift as a representation layer for ordered multichannel complex captures.

sec.title: Main Results
sec.p: The lift is deterministic under fixed channel ordering, exactly recoverable via an inverse unpacking map, and preserves capture-wide energy under grouped-lane aggregation.

sec.title: Scope and Limitations
sec.p: The contribution is representational only and does not claim new sensing physics, universal hardware advantage, or standalone performance gains from lifting alone.
